data.solutionSummary: PR widens recognition label predicate to {FF Soft Credit, Soft Credit}, adds Pledge to applications, splits query into 2 target-shaped SQL files (PSC + ASC), reshapes outputs to be NPSP-loadable directly with relationship-by-external-id headers. Pipeline/mapper/entity updated coherently.

scenario-1.evidence: dev safesql high-desert snapshot + QA unit test verification

scenario-2.rationale: User Option A: accept Pledge scope expansion

scenario-3.evidence: SQL diff confirms 2 separate files; mapper specs pass

scenario-4.evidence: QA-independent npm test run: query-regression 20/20 PASS + soft-credit-recognition 4/4 PASS

scenario-5.evidence: docs/altru-model-context.md verified contains tenant difference documentation

data.techRegression: 5/5 PASS (T1 LEFT→INNER JOIN equivalent, T2 no orphan refs, T3 specs pass, T4 re-baselined, T5 SF metadata gate out-of-scope)
data.secRegression: 6/6 PASS (no injection, no secrets, no new deps, no PII logs, no auth changes, no new injection vectors)
data.preExistingCodebaseConcern: npm audit reports 3 critical + 9 high preexisting vulnerabilities NOT introduced by this PR; follow-up at repo level
data.noDefectsRaised: PR is clean on its own surface; scope expansion accepted by user per Option A

tucsonQaActualCounts.finding: PR body framing (1,784 → 1,965 / +181 from Pledge) understates real impact (+1,804, 90% from label widening + 10% from Pledge). Code is correct; communication is misleading.
